mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad economic strength, which translates to higher expected corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent growth through 2017
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS has a rolling 36-month correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns of less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation accelerates past 2.5%, it signals to markets that the Federal Reserve will become more aggressive in its tightening cycle, increasing discount rates and causing negative equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation has been trending up towards the Fed's target
failure_condition: In periods where YoY CPI is above 2.5%, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly lower than in periods where it is below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of accelerating retail sales growth (RSAFS) and decelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicates a late-cycle peak. Strong consumption is backward-looking, while weakening housing investment is forward-looking, creating a negative signal for equities.
candidate_trigger: 6-month change in RSAFS is positive while 6-month change in HOUST is negative
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market shows signs of sensitivity to rising rates
failure_condition: Instances of the trigger condition are not followed by negative S&P 500 returns on average over the subsequent 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion defined by very low unemployment, further declines in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal an overheating labor market, leading to wage pressures that compress corporate profit margins and negatively impact equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.1% is below most estimates of NAIRU
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.5%, the correlation between monthly changes in UNRATE and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not positive and significant.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong business activity and output, directly boosting sentiment and expectations for near-term corporate performance, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Global synchronized growth narrative is prevalent in early 2018
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is consistently below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is raising the Fed Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) while real GDP growth (GDPC1) is decelerating, it signals a potential policy error of overtightening. This combination of restrictive monetary policy and slowing growth is bearish for equities.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in FEDFUNDS > 0 and quarter-over-quarter change in GDPC1 is negative
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has signaled further rate hikes for 2018
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are not, on average, negative in the 60 trading days following the simultaneous observation of a FEDFUNDS increase and a GDPC1 quarterly decline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in retail sales (RSAFS), defined as the most recent monthly value falling below its 12-month moving average, indicates a negative shock to consumer demand, which lowers revenue expectations and leads to negative stock returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly value falls below its 12-month simple moving average
failure_condition: The hit rate of the trigger (percentage of times the S&P 500 has negative 20-day returns after a trigger) is less than 55% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) increase the risk-free rate used to discount future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations. This creates a persistent negative pressure on S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The FEDFUNDS rate has risen from near-zero to 1.3%
failure_condition: The rolling 36-month correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not consistently negative.
